question_structure.context: Two identical light springs have a stiffness coefficient of $k = 100 \, \mathrm{N}/\mathrm{m}$. They are used to suspend a uniform horizontal metal rod PQ of length $L = 2 \, \mathrm{m}$ and mass $m = 0.1 \, \mathrm{kg}$ at rest. The metal rod is placed in a uniform magnetic field directed horizontally outward. When a current $I = 3 \, \mathrm{A}$ flows from end P to end Q in the metal rod, each spring elongates by $x = 2 \, \mathrm{cm}$. The acceleration due to gravity is $g = 10 \, \mathrm{m}/\mathrm{s}^{2}$.
question_structure.sub_question_1: The magnitude and direction of the Ampere force acting on the metal rod.
question_structure.sub_question_2: The magnitude of the magnetic induction of this uniform magnetic field.

sub_question_1.step_1: According to the left-hand rule, the direction of the Ampere force acting on the metal rod is vertically downward. Let the magnitude of the Ampere force on the metal rod be $F_{a}$. For a metal rod, from the equilibrium condition, we have: $mg + F_{a} = 2 k x$. Among them, $x = 2 \mathrm{cm} = 0.02 \mathrm{m}$. The solution is: $F_{a} = 3 \mathrm{N}$

sub_question_2.step_2: Let the magnitude of the magnetic induction of a uniform magnetic field be $B$, then we have: $F_{a} = B I L$. Substituting the data, we obtain: $B = 0.5 \mathrm{T}$

image_captions: The image depicts a horizontal conducting rod, labeled PQ, suspended by two vertical springs in a uniform magnetic field pointing out of the page. The rod is positioned within the magnetic field.

step_1.physical_theorem: Equilibrium of forces (static equilibrium), Left-hand rule

result_quantity.name: Direction of the Ampere force on the metal rod
Magnitude of the Ampere force on the metal rod
Magnitude of the magnetic induction of the uniform magnetic field
